company: Project Vanguard / U.S. Naval Research Laboratory
note: The rocket lost thrust moments after liftoff, fell back onto the launch pad and exploded. The satellite did not reach space.
First successful United States satellite; its data helped reveal the Van Allen radiation belts.
First solar-powered satellite and the oldest human-made object still orbiting Earth.
Confirmed and expanded measurements of the Van Allen radiation belts.
Large scientific satellite carrying instruments to study Earth's upper atmosphere and near-Earth space.
Measured charged particles in Earth's radiation belts and contributed to the discovery of the outer belt.
Failed to reach lunar orbit but travelled far into space and returned scientific measurements.
Failed to reach the Moon but climbed to about 1,530 kilometres and returned scientific data.
Failed to fly by the Moon but returned valuable measurements of Earth's radiation belts.
First communications satellite and the first use of an Atlas rocket to place a payload into orbit.
Missed its planned lunar impact, flew past the Moon and became the first spacecraft to enter heliocentric orbit.
Early weather-observation satellite designed to measure cloud-cover distribution.
Flew past the Moon farther away than planned and became the first United States spacecraft to enter heliocentric orbit.
First spacecraft stabilized in all three axes and first to send a reentry capsule back from orbit, but the capsule was not recovered.
Studied Earth's magnetosphere and transmitted the first photograph of Earth taken by a satellite in orbit.
Reached orbit, but a power-system failure prevented the planned recovery mission.
Reached orbit, but a retrorocket malfunction prevented recovery of the capsule.
